scope: Making modalities combinable, particularly focusing on the dimensionality mismatch problem.
key_concepts: When applying early fusion, practitioners often concatenate feature vectors from different modalities. For instance, concatenating an image vector $\mathbf{x}_{img} \in \mathbb{R}^{2048}$ with a tabular vector $\mathbf{x}_{tab} \in \mathbb{R}^{10}$.

This creates a dimensionality imbalance: the high-dimensional modality (e.g., image or text) will mathematically dominate distance-based or gradient-based models, drowning out the low-dimensional tabular signal.

To balance representations, one must apply dimensionality reduction (e.g., PCA or bottleneck layers) to the high-dimensional modality, or project the low-dimensional modality into a higher space using learned embeddings.
Scaling is also critical: features from different modalities often have vastly different ranges and variances, requiring robust normalization before concatenation.

definitions.definition: Praktik menyelaraskan jumlah fitur antar modalitas agar tidak ada satu modalitas dominan yang mengalahkan sinyal dari modalitas lain saat penggabungan.
Proses menggabungkan dua atau lebih vektor fitur menjadi satu vektor agregat yang lebih panjang.

examples: Combining a 768-dimensional text embedding with a 5-dimensional tabular profile. Without dimensionality reduction on the text (e.g., via Truncated SVD to 20 dimensions), the 5 tabular features might be ignored by the algorithm.
